multiplechoiceresponse.label: Looking at the Grad-CAM visualizations across the top-5 predicted categories (village, islet, beach_house, coast, harbor), which conclusion is not justified by these plots?

choicegroup.choice: The model relies on different image regions depending on which scene label is being evaluated.
The highlighted regions correspond to visually meaningful parts of the scene, such as shoreline, water, and buildings.
The highlighted regions are the visual evidence the model associates most strongly with each class.
The highlighted regions are the true physical causes of the scene category in the real world.

div.p: Grad-CAM highlights regions that are important for the model’s prediction, not regions that are causally responsible for the scene in the real world. While the heatmaps are often intuitive and align with human expectations, they reflect learned associations in the network rather than causal explanations.
